target: expensive (software) transcendental elementwise: tanh/sinh/cosh/asinh/acosh/atanh + bessel_i0e/i1e (over large dense-F64), bench eval/tanh_1m_f64
lever: Extend pass120's threaded transcendental elementwise to the hyperbolic / inverse-hyperbolic / Bessel ops (software-implemented, compute-bound). Measured-and-EXCLUDED the cheap hardware intrinsics exp/log/sin/cos/tan (only ~1.9x at 1M — memory-bound, with near-threshold over-spawn risk), keeping them on the serial path. Raised the shared threshold to 1<<18 (262144) so the cheaper kept ops amortize the thread fan-out.
isomorphism: BIT-EXACT (each element independent, out[i]=op(src[i])). Covered by lgamma_parallel_bit_identical (the shared eval_unary_elementwise_parallel path, pass120) + tanh/sinh/cosh value tests (6 pass). Cheap-op serial path unchanged.
decision: Route the EXPENSIVE software transcendentals (tanh 4.6x and the hyperbolic/inverse/Bessel family); leave the cheap hardware intrinsics (exp/log/sin/cos/tan, ~1.9x, memory-bound) serial — they don't clear the 2x bar and risk near-threshold over-spawn.
score: impact 5 (tanh = ubiquitous activation; sinh/cosh/asinh/acosh/atanh/bessel common in scientific ML) x confidence 5 (bit-exact, cheap ops measured-and-excluded) / effort 2 = 12.5
tests: fj-lax tanh 6/6; lib transcendental + lgamma_parallel_bit_identical pass; clippy -D warnings clean.
bench_note: Temporary exp/tanh benches in foreign-dirty lax_baseline.rs (restored, not committed). Serial baselines via in-place PARALLEL_MIN_ELEMS=usize::MAX toggle. Only arithmetic.rs committed.
